Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

CTN 4, 046 [Šumma Alu Varia][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

* ⸢KI.MIN⸣ ina ⸢UGU⸣ [...]

(o 1') (If) ditto, on top of [...].

o 2'2'

* ⸢KI⸣.MIN ina UGU x AN x x x [...] ⸢LU*?⸣ BIRaḫ [...]

(o 2') (If) ditto, on top of ... [...] ... will be scattered; [...].

o 3'3'

* ⸢KI⸣.MIN ina ⸢UGU?⸣ x x x x-⸢MEŠ*⸣ ina A AN x ⸢LU*?⸣ E₂ BI x (x) x [x (x)]

(o 3') (If) ditto, on top of ... in ... that house ... [...].

o 4'4'

* ⸢KI⸣.MIN ina ⸢UGU? SIG₄⸣ E₂ NA IGI E₂ BI BIRaḫ

(o 4') (If) ditto is seen on top of the brickwork of a man's house: that household will be scattered.

o 5'5'

* KI.⸢MIN⸣ ina ⸢UGU? LU₂⸣ id-mu-um E₂ BI ZAḪ₂

(o 5') (If) ditto moans on top of a man: that house will be destroyed.

o 6'6'

* ⸢KI.MIN ina* E₂ LU₂⸣ ip-pa-ris-ma E₃ E₂ BI ina-an-ziq na-aš-par₂-ti dAMAR?.UTU?1

(o 6') (If) ditto is blocked in a man's house but goes out: that household will be annoyed; a message from Marduk.

o 7'7'

* ⸢KI⸣.[MIN ...] NI ana UGU NA ŠUB!ut ⸢EN⸣ E₂ UŠ₂-ma E₂ BI BIRaḫ

(o 7') (If) ditto ... falls on top of a man: the house's owner will die and that household will be scattered.

o 8'8'

* ⸢KI.MIN⸣ [ina] ⸢ap*?⸣-ti E₂ NA is-⸢si⸣ ti-bu kaš-du

(o 8') (If) ditto cries out [at] the window of a man's house: successful attack.

o 9'9'

* ⸢KI⸣.MIN ina UGU E₂ LU₂ DU.DU <<IN>> E₂ BI in!(LUGAL-)na-qar

(o 9') (If) ditto roams around over a man's house: that house will be demolished.

o 10'10'

* KI.MIN ina E₂ DINGIR IGI E₂ DINGIR ⸢BI⸣ ŠUBdi

(o 10') (If) ditto is seen in a temple: that temple will be abandoned.

o 11'11'

* ⸢KI.MIN⸣ ina UR₃ E₂.GAL IGI E₂.GAL ⸢BI?⸣ ŠUBdi

(o 11') (If) ditto is seen on the palace's roof: that palace will be abandoned.

o 12'12'

* ⸢KI.MIN⸣ ina UGU NA is-si ti-bu kaš-du

(o 12') (If) ditto cries out over a man: successful attack.

o 13'13'

* KI.MIN GE₆ ina na-me-e it-ta-mar <<A?>> mim-ma-šu₂-nu ŠUB-⸢MEŠ-ma⸣

(o 13') (If) ditto is always seen at night in the steppe: they will abandon all their (belongings) and the mind of their troops will be confused; ... [...].

o 14'14'

UMUŠ EREN₂?-MEŠ-šu₂-nu ip-pa-ra-ar₂ x [...]

o 15'15'

šum-ma qa₂-du-u₂ im-ti-du!(DI-)ma ŠA₃!?-ME-šu₂ [...]

(o 15') If owls become numerous and in their centres [...] keep settling down: the meadow ... [...].

o 16'16'

⸢ir?⸣-ta-nab-bi-ṣu A.GAR₃ x [...]

o 17'17'

šum-ma qa₂-⸢du?⸣-u₂ ina A.GAR₃ im-ti-du ana [...]

(o 17') If owls become numerous in the meadow: on [...].

o 18'18'

[*] dNIN.⸢NINNA⸣ [(x)]-⸢la?⸣-tu₂ ina ŠA₃ ⸢IRI⸣ [...]

(o 18') (If) a harrier-hawk ... [...] in the centre of the city [...].

o 19'19'

[*] KI.MIN<(dNIN.⸢NINNA⸣)> ana ŠA₃ IRI KU₄-⸢ma⸣ DU.DU [...]

(o 19') (If) ditto (a harrier-hawk) enters the centre of the city and roams around [...].

o 20'20'

[*] KI.MIN<(dNIN.⸢NINNA⸣)> ka-la u₄-mi ana ŠA₃ IRI ⸢KU₄⸣ [...]

(o 20') (If) ditto (a harrier-hawk) enters the centre of the city (for) the whole day [...].

o 21'21'

[*] KI.MIN<(dNIN.⸢NINNA⸣)> ka-la u₄-mi ana ŠA₃ IRI ⸢KU₄?⸣ [...]

(o 21') (If) ditto (a harrier-hawk) enters the centre of the city (for) the whole day [...].

o 22'22'

[*] KI.MIN<(dNIN.⸢NINNA⸣)> ka-la u₄-mi KI.MIN<(ana)> ŠA₃ IRI ⸢KU₄?⸣ [...] ana GE₆ [...]

(o 22') (If) ditto (a harrier-hawk) ditto (enters the centre of the city) (for) the whole day [...] for the night [...].

o 23'23'

[*] KI.MIN<(dNIN.⸢NINNA⸣)> E₂ LU₂ u₂-⸢te?⸣-[...]

(o 23') (If) ditto (a harrier-hawk) ... a man's house [...].

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...] x [...]

(r 1') [...] ... [...]

r 2'2'

[...] ⸢E₂⸣ [...] ⸢SU? KU₄⸣ [...]

(r 2') [...] enters [...] the house ... [...]

r 3'3'

[...] E₂ ⸢NA⸣ [...] ⸢GUB*.GUB?⸣az [...]

(r 3') [...] keeps standing around [...] the man's house [...]

r 4'4'

[...] GIŠ x [...] ⸢KUR*?⸣ TI* [...]

(r 4') [...] ... [...] ... [...]

r 5'5'

[...] x [...] x KA x [...]

(r 5') [...] ... [...] ... [...]

r 6'6'

⸢KI.MIN⸣ [...] ⸢NA?⸣ [...] ⸢LI* mi*-na*-ti*-šu*-nu*⸣ [...]

(r 6') Ditto [...] a man [...] their limbs [...].

r 7'7'

x [x] ⸢MI⸣ [...] x [...]

(r 7') ... [...] ... [...] ... [...].

r 8'8'

KI.MIN x ⸢E₂⸣ x [...] x x [...]

(r 8') Ditto ... the house ... [...] ... [...].

r 9'9'

KI.MIN ana [...] x [...] x [...]

(r 9') Ditto to [...] ... [...] ... [...].

r 10'10'

KI.MIN ina [x (x)] x x E₂ LU₂ ⸢U₂⸣ x MIN? ana ⸢E₂ NA⸣ [...]

(r 10') Ditto in [...] ... the man's house ... to the man's house [...].

r 11'11'

KI.MIN ina ⸢UGU?⸣ LU₂ ⸢IGI?⸣ [...] KU₄*-ma* DIŠ* A* u₂-ṣa-bi-ru* ⸢UKU₂*⸣ [...]

(r 11') Ditto is seen in front of a man [...] enters and they twitter ...: he will become poor.

r 12'12'

* [...] IGI x-MEŠ [...] x x x ⸢ZUM?⸣ LU₂ BI ŠU x [...]

(r 12') (If) [...] ... [...] ... that man, the hand ... [...]

r 13'13'

šum-ma x x U LI⸢mušen*⸣ x [...] x GUB.GUBaz ni!-šu [x (x)] LU₂ [x (x)]

(r 13') If a ...-bird keeps standing around ... [...] ...: the people [...] the man [...].

r 14'14'

KI.MIN ina? x [...] E₂ NA x [x (x)] KU₄ ŠUBti x [...] E₂

(r 14') Ditto enters in ... [...] of the man's house ... [...]: epidemic; ... [...] of the house.

r 15'15'

KI.MIN ina x [...] ⸢ŠA₃*?⸣ [x] x RA BI x ki!(KU-)sal*-li DU x x ⸢E₂ BI⸣ BIR

(r 15') Ditto in ... [...] ... he will go in the ... of the court; ... that household will be scattered.

r 16'16'

KI.MIN ina ap*-ti E₂ NA qin-na [iq]-⸢ta⸣-na-an E₂ BI ŠUB!?⸢di⸣

(r 16') Ditto makes a nest at the window of a man's house: that house will be abandoned.

r 17'17'

KI.MIN ša₂ ka-li mim-mu-[šu₂?] x ⸢it⸣-ta-mar IRI BI ŠUBdi

(r 17') Ditto is seen from all his belongings ...: that city will be abandoned.

r 18'18'

KI.MIN ina <<MU>> ḫu-ur LU₂ KA x GUB*.⸢GUB⸣az E₂ BI BIRaḫ2

(r 18') Ditto ... on a man's limb and stands; that household will be scattered.

r 19'19'

KI.MIN ina mu-ut ḫu-ur ⸢LU₂⸣ [...] x x GUB.GUBaz E₂ BI BIRaḫ

(r 19') Ditto ...stands on the front part of a man's limb [...] ... will stand; that house will be scattered.

r 20'20'

šum-ma it-ti-du-u₂ ina nap-ru-ši [...] ⸢E₂⸣ ŠA₃ IRI BI ŠUBdi

(r 20') If a francolin, while flying, [...]: [...] of the house; the centre of that city will be abandoned.

r 21'21'

⸢šum⸣-[ma ...] x x MA ZA x AN!? [...] ŠA₃ IRI BI [ŠUB]di

(r 21') If [...] ... becomes strong and ... [...] ... the centre of that city will be abandoned.

(rest of reverse missing)

1The reading of AMAR.UTU at the end of the line is uncertain.

2the scribe probably anticipates the next line by writing the sign MU.
